perturbation.original_text: 2.13 "Specifications" shall mean the standards established in writing by the Parties for the characteristics, quality, and quality control testing of Product, and its constituents, components, and packaging, as further described in Section 4.4 below and Schedule 2, and as in effect from time to time.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.13 "Specifications" shall mean the standards established in writing unilaterally by CPDC for the characteristics, quality, and quality control testing of Product, and its constituents, components, and packaging, as further described in Section 4.4 below and Schedule 2, and as in effect from time to time.
perturbation.explanation: This change alters the definition of 'Specifications' to mean that they are established unilaterally by CPDC, instead of being agreed upon by both parties. This contradicts the implicit understanding of a supply agreement where specifications are usually a mutually agreed-upon standard. This change impacts Section 3.6 where Batch Testing is mentioned, because it implies that Fusion's right to test against mutually agreed specifications is undermined.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.6 Batch Testing. FUSION shall have the right but not the obligation to conduct any Batch testing [***] or investigation it determines to be of value to determine compliance of Product with the Specifications and/or pursuant to any other standard imposed by law. A Batch shall be deemed to be acceptable if, upon testing, it meets the Specifications, relevant cGMP standards, all other applicable laws, rules and regulations (and any additional tests as agreed by the Parties). If either Party discovers that a Batch does not meet the Specifications, relevant cGMP standards or other applicable laws, rules or regulations, then the discovering Party shall promptly communicate with the other Party. All warranty obligations of CPDC with respect to a particular Batch shall cease and have no effect to the extent that any defect in such Batch arises from abuse, misuse, alteration, mishandling, improper storage or gross negligence by FUSION or FUSION'S employees, representatives, agents, suppliers or carriers, or defects in Precursor materials furnished by FUSION which are used in the production of such Batch.
perturbation.justification: The change allows CPDC to unilaterally define 'Specifications,' which directly contradicts FUSION's right, stated in Section 3.6, to conduct batch testing to determine compliance with 'the Specifications.' If CPDC unilaterally sets the specifications, FUSION's right to test against mutually agreed upon specifications is undermined, creating a clear conflict and undermining the basis for determining batch acceptability.

perturbation.original_text: 3.3 Shipping: All deliveries of Products shall be Free Carrier shipping point [***]. For greater certainty, [***] shall take ownership of and bear all risk of loss of or damage to the Products at the origin of the shipment.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.3 Shipping: All deliveries of Products shall be Free Carrier shipping point [***]. For greater certainty, CPDC shall retain ownership and bear all risk of loss of or damage to the Products until delivered to FUSION's specified location.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the original allocation of risk during shipping. Originally, the Buyer (FUSION) assumes ownership and risk at the origin of the shipment. The modified version shifts the risk and ownership to the Supplier (CPDC) until delivery. This change contradicts section 4.7 Recalls, and creates an in-text contradiction because if CPDC retains ownership and risk of product until FUSION location, and is faulty, CPDC is liable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.7 Recalls. FUSION shall notify CPDC promptly if any Product is the subject of a recall or correction (a "Recall"), and FUSION and/or its designee shall have sole responsibility for the handling and disposition of such Recall. [***]. (a) In the event that CPDC disputes FUSION'S determination that the fault is due to CPDC and/or to its employees or agents, the Parties will select a mutually agreeable outside consulting firm which will be instructed to review the applicable information and data and to confirm or dissent from FUSION'S determination. If the consulting firm confirms FUSION'S determination, CPDC will pay the fees of such consulting firm. If the consulting firm dissents from FUSION'S determination, CPDC will not have the obligations set forth herein with respect to the Recall and FUSION will pay the fees of such consulting firm. (b) FUSION and/or its designee shall maintain records of all sales, shipping records of Product and customers in sufficient detail to adequately administer a Recall for the period of time as required by applicable law and regulation. CPDC's Shipping Records of Product will be given to Fusion on request such that Fusion can maintain the records and, if such a request is made, will be delivered within a timeframe to be detailed within Quality Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The modified text in Section 3.3 shifts the ownership and risk of loss for the Products to CPDC until delivery. This directly contradicts Section 4.7, which states that FUSION has sole responsibility for handling and disposition of recalls. If CPDC retains ownership and risk until delivery, as per the modified Section 3.3, then CPDC should be responsible for recalls, which creates a conflict with Section 4.7's allocation of recall responsibility to FUSION. This ambiguity creates an in-text contradiction that would likely lead to disputes.

perturbation.original_text: 5.2 CPDC Limited Product Warranty. CPDC hereby provides a limited product warranty, and accordingly does warrant for each Batch, that the Product shipped will (i) conform with the Specifications, (ii) be manufactured, tested, processed, packed and prepared for shipment in accordance with cGMPs, and (iii) be free from defects in material and workmanship for the period from the date of manufacture to the expiry date set out on each Unit of Product packed and prepared for shipment.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.2 CPDC Limited Product Warranty. CPDC hereby provides no product warranty, and accordingly does not warrant for each Batch, that the Product shipped will (i) conform with the Specifications, (ii) be manufactured, tested, processed, packed and prepared for shipment in accordance with cGMPs, and (iii) be free from defects in material and workmanship for the period from the date of manufacture to the expiry date set out on each Unit of Product packed and prepared for shipment.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation completely removes CPDC's product warranty. This directly contradicts the indemnification clause in Section 10.1, which holds CPDC responsible for losses arising from failures to conform to specifications. With no warranty, it is unclear how the indemnification clause can be enforced or what standards apply, leading to conflicting obligations and an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10.1 By CPDC. CPDC shall defend, indemnify and hold FUSION and its Affiliates and sublicensees, and each of their respective directors, officers and employees, harmless from and against any and all damages, liabilities, costs and expenses (including the reasonable costs and expenses of lawyers and other professionals) (collectively "Losses") incurred by FUSION in connection with any claim, demand, action or other proceeding (each, a "Claim") by a third party (excluding FUSION'S Affiliates and sublicensees), to the extent such Losses arise out of (a) failure of the Product delivered under this Agreement to conform to the Specifications; (b) CPDC's breach of this Agreement, including without limitation any failure of its representations and warranties set forth in Section 5.1 or 5.2 to have been accurate when made or any breach of the covenants set forth in this Agreement; or (c) the gross negligence or intentional misconduct of CPDC or any of its Affiliates, or any of their respective directors, officers, employees, provided CPDC will not have an indemnification obligation with respect to any Claim to the extent that FUSION has an indemnification obligation under Section 10.2.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly removes the product warranty provided by CPDC, stating "CPDC hereby provides no product warranty". This directly contradicts section 10.1, which holds CPDC liable for losses arising from failures of the product to conform to specifications. If there is no warranty, the basis for CPDC's indemnification obligation under 10.1(a) becomes unclear, creating an in-text contradiction and potential unenforceability. Furthermore, 10.1(b) refers to breaches of warranties in section 5.2, which is now void, leading to a contradiction.
